entry.title: Measuring Business Outcomes in SAP Cloud ERP
entry.content:
This blog's intent is to expand on my previous one: Sample value drivers for effective Business Value Realization of your Public Cloud Solution.

Now that you have identified the value drivers and specific solution capabilities that can bring you additional business value, how do you go about knowing if you are improving? How do you go about measuring the value of your improvements without creating a sophisticated value driver baseline or measuring the value drivers in a qualitative way? In this blog, I also want to provide you with a simple and pragmatic approach using SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition standard applications as a source to get some information to establish the baseline (you might still need to do some calculations if you want to measure the exact value driver), but by using standard apps you can use them as an alternative to see if your business is improving, which, in the end, without a lot of effort, is what you want to do—measure improvement.  Obviously, if you want real value driver measurement you can also play with analytics in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition to either create your own KPI, reports, multidimensional reports, stories, or booklets, to make an exact calculation of the value driver.

Remember, the approach I am suggesting here is not the only approach, as SAP has other services like Value Management and/or other tools like SAP Signavio Process Intelligence, etc., that can help you with this exercise as well. In my previous blog, I covered very briefly how SAP Signavio Process Intelligence — Drive more business outcomes out of your SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition – A Recommendation Guide

Now let's recap on the different areas we suggested in my previous blog:

  • Reduce selling, general, and administrative expenses
  • Increase revenue
  • Decrease cost of goods sold (COGS)
  • Improve working capital
  • Improve pre-tax margin

Sample value drivers by improvement area

Selling, general, and administrative expenses

Ok, let’s start with lowering our selling, general, and administrative expenses. Here I recommended the following value drivers in my last blog: “Reduce account receivable management costs” and “Reduce invoice error rate.”

Reduce account receivable management costs – This value driver measures the total cost of accounts receivable as a percentage of revenue, so yes, you can use the system and create some specific analytics that can collect several general ledger accounts for your accounts receivable and then compare with your revenue to calculate this. So, somehow this value driver requires some information and calculation to come up with the total cost of the accounts receivable function and compare with your revenue. But my advice is that you instead look to see improvements metrics on accounts receivable to measure the real impact on improvements out of the box and not only the cost. For that, I recommend you use the Accounts Receivable Overview App, which will provide you with other KPIs to improve your AR function, like AR aging analysis, Top 10 Debtors, Exceeded Credit Limit, etc. This will provide you a meaningful measurement out of the box to measure improvements you can easily compare every time you use this app.

Accounts Receivables Overview

For the Reduce invoice error rate, as we are focusing on Accounts Receivable, I suggest you use the Overdue Receivables in Dispute and select some of the reasons like “Price Difference.” While this doesn’t give you an automatic error rate, you can use disputed amounts with the price difference reason, for example, to set the baseline to compare. Otherwise, you will need to create specific analytics that calculate all invoices and allow you to look at dispute cases over a specific time period, which complicates the measurement, unless you want to run it a bit more qualitatively.

Overdue Receivables in Dispute – filter by Reason: Price Difference

Revenue

On Revenue, we selected two different value drivers: Reduce manufacturing cycle time and Reduce revenue loss due to fulfillment issues.

Here I am recommending two apps that can provide you with this information. For reducing manufacturing cycle time, you can use the Production Execution Duration app. This app will be able to provide you with the plan and actual information by work center, which you can use to measure your improvements. You can then filter this by material, planning plant, production plan, and work center.

Production Execution Duration

Now for the Reduce revenue loss due to fulfillment issues, you can use the Sales Order Fulfillment – Analyze and Resolve app.

This app will help you look at current fulfillment issues and try to resolve them.

Sales Order Fulfillment – Analyze and Resolve

Cost of Good Sold

For the two selected value drivers, Reduce total manufacturing costs and Reduce asset maintenance costs, we have two apps that we suggest to use. The first one is the Analyze Production Costs – Event Based app, to help you measure the total manufacturing cost. There might be other apps you may want to use depending on your specific production process.

Production Costs – Event Based

The second one to help measure the reduction of maintenance costs is the app, Maintenance Order Costs Plan and Actuals. This app will help you to execute these tasks.

Maintenance Oder Costs – Plan/Actuals

Working Capital

In working capital, we suggested two value drivers: reduce sales days outstanding and reduce days in inventory.

To measure these two value drivers, you can use the following applications. For sales days outstanding, you can use the app “Days Sales Outstanding – 12 months,” which can provide you with a quick way to measure this value driver. The app provides you with the option to see this by period, by customer, and by company code.

Days Sales Outstanding – 12 months

The app Inventory Turnover Analysis can provide you with the reduction of days in inventory.

Inventory Turnover Analysis

Pre-tax Margin

In the pre-tax margin area, we selected two value drivers: improve on-time delivery performance and reduce uncollectible accounts receivable write-offs.

We could use the Delivery Performance app to help improve on-time delivery performance. We have two apps to choose from: Delivery Performance as Requested and Delivery Performance as Committed. Both apps look similar; the difference is one presents the information as requested and the other as committed. We also have an app that helps you analyze delivery performance – shipped as planned.

Delivery Performance as Requested

And to reduce uncollectible accounts receivable write-offs, we suggest using the app Cash Collector Tracker – Account Receivables. The app allows you to see this by Company Code, by Accounting Clerk, and by top 10 customers.

Collector Tracker – Account Receivables

I hope these out-of-the-box apps provide you an alternative to complex calculations to identify measurements for your value drivers as you drive improvements to your business.

I also want to thank my colleagues Edna Garcia and Jens Klawiter from Cloud ERP Product Success for the joint discussions on this topic and the checks we executed together in SAP Cloud ERP.

Keep in mind that solution capabilities are expanding from traditional functional scope to also include SAP Business AI, so SAP will continue to add more in this area. As in my previous blogs, remember, this is not the only tool that can be used; SAP has other tools such as SAP Signavio, SAP WalkMe, SAP LeanIX, and SAP Cloud ALM that can be used in tandem to help drive more business value from your SAP subscription.

I encourage you to discuss these points with your SAP Customer Success Manager, SAP Account Executive, or SAP Partner. They may be able to provide you with additional recommendations and guidance.

Your insights and opinions are valuable, so please don't hesitate to contribute by commenting on this blog.

Follow us via @SAP and #S4HANA or follow @Marco_Valencia (SAP Business Suite Customer Success Management).

entry.title: Why Now Is the Time to Rethink Your ERP: Understanding SAP Cloud ERP and the Power of a Fresh Start
entry.content:
For many organizations, SAP ERP has been the backbone of business operations for years, sometimes decades. These systems have supported growth and stability, but they were implemented in a different era of business needs, regulatory requirements, and technological possibilities.

Today, the pace of change has accelerated dramatically. New compliance demands, rapid market shifts, AI-driven work models, and global expansion requirements are stretching older SAP ERP releases beyond what they were originally designed to support.

The mounting pressure to modernize

Customers running older SAP ERP releases increasingly face challenges such as:

  • Growing operational risk from aging, highly customized systems
  • Manual workarounds and resource drain, limiting IT’s ability to innovate
  • Technical debt that makes upgrades costly and complex
  • Rapidly approaching maintenance deadlines, increasing urgency to act
  • Missed opportunities for automation and AI

In short: maintaining the status quo is becoming more complex and more expensive.

Why more companies are considering a fresh start with cloud ERP?

Modern cloud ERP isn’t just a new deployment option; it’s a new operating model. It delivers simplicity, continuous innovation, and the ability to scale without the burden of heavy custom code or technical upgrades. Across industries, organizations are seeing value in five key areas:

  1. Agility and scalability
    Adopt best‑practice processes, respond faster to change, and scale operations globally without infrastructure constraints.
  2. Cost efficiency and predictable operations
    Shift from hardware ownership and upgrade projects to a subscription model with predictable spend.
  3. Continuous innovation
    Receive new capabilities, including embedded analytics, automation, and AI, through automated update cycles multiple times per year.
  4. Strong security and resilience
    Benefit from enterprise‑grade security, global SLAs, and always‑current compliance delivered by SAP.
  5. Faster time to value
    With a fit‑to‑standard approach and ready‑to‑run best practices, organizations achieve results quickly reducing implementation effort and accelerating ROI.

A Cloud ERP built for existing SAP customers

SAP Cloud ERP helps companies running SAP ERP on-premise to move forward with confidence:

  • Run in the cloud with a modern, intuitive user experience, embedded intelligence, and continuous updates
  • Get innovation moving with global localization coverage, seamless extensibility, and rapid delivery of new features
  • Move with confidence using SAP Activate, migration accelerators, and tools like the Data Migration Cockpit, reducing complexity while preserving familiar structures and terminology

Many customers choose to start fresh, leaving behind outdated custom code and organizational complexity while keeping the essentials that still provide value.

Real outcomes from real customers

Companies adopting SAP Cloud ERP report outcomes such as:

  • Faster financial close cycles
  • Reduced customization footprint
  • Increased automation and visibility
  • Improved user satisfaction
  • Lower operational and infrastructure costs
  • Faster onboarding of subsidiaries and acquisitions

Most importantly, they gain the ability to innovate continuously, without waiting for the next big upgrade.

 

Ready to understand what Cloud ERP really means for your organization?

If you’re running an older SAP ERP release, now is the right moment to explore what a modern cloud ERP could unlock for your business, especially with time‑to‑value becoming a critical decision factor.

We invite you to join our upcoming webinar:

Understanding SAP Cloud ERP: Public Cloud Concepts & Operating Model

You’ll learn:

  • Why organizations running older SAP ERP systems are rethinking their ERP strategy
  • What “public cloud ERP” really means in practice
  • How a SaaS operating model delivers continuous innovation
  • How SAP supports a smooth transition through proven tools and methodology
  • What a fresh‑start journey can look like for both business and IT teams

The session gives you a clear, practical foundation without spoiling the deeper insights we’ll cover live.

Register here: https://events.sap.com/transformation/en_us/home.html

entry.title: IAM Release Activities: Restriction Category Appears Orange Despite No Visible Empty Restrictions
entry.content:
In this blog post, I would like to share an issue we encountered in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud during the IAM Release Activities for the 2602 upgrade. This information may help others facing similar inconsistencies in business role maintenance. 

Issue: 

During the post-upgrade role maintenance, we aimed to maintain all empty restrictions using the Maintain Business Roles app. 

However, we encountered cases like the example below where: 

  • The restriction category appeared in orange, indicating empty restriction fields. 
  • Opening the restriction category showed no visible empty fields. 
  • Under Restriction and Values, the system displayed "No data". 

 

Root Cause: 

The orange indicator is correct: the restriction category does contain empty restriction fields, even if they are not shown initially. 


Solution: 

Use the Add button to display and insert the missing restriction fields. After adding them, they can be maintained as required. 

 

Summary: 

Not all restrictions are displayed automatically. If the system indicates missing or empty restrictions, manually adding them may resolve the inconsistency.

entry.title: AI and UX sessions at SAP Sapphire 2026 that I’m most excited about
entry.content:
SAP Sapphire is where SAP makes its big announcements about its vision for the future of ERP and business applications. This includes showing new products, presenting customers who have transformed their business using SAP’s solutions, and highlighting new technologies that will shape the future.

For 2026, AI will continue to dominate the event. AI will change the way that people consume enterprise software through chat-based interfaces and screens that generate on-the-fly based on what you want to do. Eventually.

In this post, I’ll share the sessions at Sapphire and ASUG Annual Conference that I’m most excited about. For the full menu, you can browse the session catalog. My preferences are, of course, shaped by my role on the Cloud ERP UX Foundation team. I’ll organize by date so you can plan your agenda. You can also use the browser to search by topic or speaker if that makes it easier for you. Some are even available on-demand. Let’s go!

Monday, May 11, 2026

Day / Time / Session ID

Session title and description

Speakers

Monday
10am – 5pm
ASUG102

Unlocking Business Value with AI: Strategy, Security, and the Road Ahead

Gain a comprehensive and practical overview of SAP Business AI. This workshop will examine core strategies, security frameworks, and future trends while exploring real-world customer stories. The session will conclude with an expert panel and a hands-on AI road mapping session.

Many experts from SAP

Monday
2:00pm – 2:20pm
ERP2143

The future of enterprise: Innovations in SAP Cloud ERP Private

Discover how SAP Cloud ERP Private solutions enable the next era of enterprise transformation. This introductory session explores how cutting-edge artificial intelligence, trusted data, and integrated applications work in harmony, creating smarter, faster, and more resilient businesses within the security and flexibility of your private cloud.

Nicole Smythe, SAP

Vinay V, SAP

Monday
2:30pm – 2:50pm
CXP2149

Legacy to legendary: The AI-enhanced path

Explore the strategic road map for moving from legacy on-premises systems to a cloud-native, AI-enhanced architecture. Discover how a clean core approach helps reduce technical debt, accelerate innovation, and position your organization to use AI for greater efficiency and agility.

 

Monday
3:30pm – 3:50pm
PUX1349

Innovations and value that SAP Business AI offers

Learn how you can find the best AI features for your use cases, and understand the business value and associated cost with our self-service tools.

Pascal Mauge, SAP

 

Tuesday, May 12, 2026

Day / Time / Session ID

Session title and description

Speakers

Tuesday
10:30 am - 11:15 am PUX1228

Creating better user experiences with AI-assisted SAP Fiori apps | PUX1228

New AI features in SAP Fiori apps boost user productivity and speed onboarding. Starting with the “My Home” page and drilling down into typical SAP Fiori apps, see how AI makes it easier to find information, enter data, automate tasks, and make informed business decisions. Explore how conversational interactions make SAP solutions more intuitive.

 

Peter Spielvogel, SAP

Tuesday
11:00 am - 11:20 am PUX1346

Great features don’t guarantee success: Experience does

Explore SAP Business Suite in the cloud to discover how you can shape the experience of the product for stellar adoption success.

Daniel Becker, SAP

Tuesday
11:30 am - 12:15 pm FOU1861

ASUG Community Conversation: Artificial intelligence

Explore platform strategy, embedded and generative AI use cases, integration patterns, and road map priorities with experts in AI and SAP Business Technology Platform. Discuss architecture, data readiness, and clean core alignment while exchanging lessons learned with peers advancing AI innovation.

John Astill, SAP

Tuesday
11:30 am - 12:15 pm ERP1622

SAP ECC to SAP S/4HANA: What’s new, why change, and why now?

 Learn from more than 2,400 cloud ERP projects as we navigate the shift from SAP ERP Central Component (SAP ECC) to SAP S/4HANA. Compare greenfield, brownfield, and selective data transitions to find the right path. Discover the key differences in SAP S/4HANA, the value of moving now, and how to accelerate AI adoption with modern technology.

 

Esther Vrieze, SAP

Bert Schulze, SAP

Tuesday
11:30 am - 12:15 pm BTM2345

Why ERP transformations fail: The change management gap

Organizations expect operational efficiency and digital capabilities from their SAP solution transformations, yet some ERP programs fail to realize full value. The missing link is often adoption. See how integrating organizational change management early in the SAP lifecycle reduces resistance, accelerates adoption, and improves business outcomes.

 

Kara Beck, Moog Inc.

Tuesday
1:00 pm - 1:45 pm ERP1864

ASUG Community Conversation: Cloud ERP and SAP S/4HANA

In this interactive ASUG Community Conversation for professionals in cloud ERP and SAP S/4HANA, explore strategies across migration, operation, and innovation—featuring our road maps, clean core and AI insights, and customer lessons learned. Exchange practical guidance with peers and shape future discussions through open dialogue.

Peter Spielvogel, SAP

Nicole Smythe, SAP

Tuesday
1:00 pm - 1:20 pm JOU2645

The future of enterprise applications and AI experiences

Unlock real value from agentic AI with insights from SAP experts and peers. Explore what transformation means today and how you can scale AI across the business, overcome common challenges, govern risk, and build an AI-first enterprise that drives measurable top- and bottom-line impact.

 

Tuesday
2:00 pm - 2:45 pm ERP1178

2026 and beyond: Discover the latest innovations in SAP Cloud ERP Private 

Running your ERP in the cloud allows you to benefit from innovations at your own pace. Explore recent AI and other functional developments planned for the next releases of SAP Cloud ERP Private solutions to help you prepare for adoption, build your road map, and accelerate time to value.

Maura Hameroff, SAP

Rudolf Hois, SAP

Tuesday
3:00 pm - 3:45 pm FOU1559

Build enterprise-grade pro-code agents on SAP Business Technology Platform

Unlock the power of autonomous agents with enterprise-grade guarantees. Get technical tips on building pro-code agents on SAP Business Technology Platform that balance innovation with responsibility. Learn to use the Model Context Protocol (MCP) for smooth tool integration while supporting security, ethical AI behavior, and regulatory compliance.

Evgenii Skrebtcov, SAP

Tim Back, SAP

Tuesday
3:00 pm - 3:45 pm ERP1834

Extend SAP S/4HANA Cloud while keeping a clean core | ERP1834

Extend the capabilities of SAP S/4HANA Cloud to address your business needs. Use AI to generate projects in the ABAP programming language and SAP Cloud Application Programming Model from your business requirements. Build SAP Fiori apps based on your written or graphical description, and use natural language filtering to find information in them.

Katja Zoch, SAP

Tuesday
3:30 pm - 3:50 pm JOU2646

AI as the new UI

The interface is disappearing, and that's the point. As AI becomes the primary way people interact with enterprise software, the question is no longer how to design better screens but how to deliver better outcomes. Intent replaces navigation, conversation replaces configuration, and the most powerful user experience is the one you barely notice.

 

Tuesday
4:00 pm - 4:20 pm PUX1192

SAP's user experience strategy for an AI-powered web and mobile future

Get an overview of how SAP is helping business users get their work done more easily and with better outcomes: using the Joule solution and embedded AI to take UX to the next level, providing a consistent and integrated suite experience, and leveraging our user research framework to meet users' needs. Also see how you can create great UX yourself.

Arin Bhowmick, SAP

 

Wednesday, May 13, 2026

Day / Time / Session ID

Session title and description

Speakers

Wednesday
10:30 am – 11:15 am SAP1623

SAP ECC to SAP Business Suite: Migration strategies and cloud insights

Master the direct transition from SAP ERP Central Component to SAP Business Suite using proven best practices. Compare public, private, and on-premises deployments to define key decision drivers. Learn to navigate annual releases, leverage essential migration tools, and identify the evolving skills needed for a successful, seamless cloud journey.

Esther Vrieze, SAP

Bert Schultze, SAP

Wednesday
10:30 am – 11:15 am PUX1819

SAP Fiori in action: How modern UX drives efficiency in cloud ERP

Get an overview of how the SAP Fiori user experience (UX) helps users get their work done more easily and efficiently. See how we are using AI today in applications and with the Joule solution. Understand our road map for revolutionizing UX, how AI changes the idea of what an application is, and what all this means for you.

Thomas Reiss, SAP

Wednesday
11:30 am – 11:50 am SUS1523

AI that helps you operate your business

As sustainability shifts from reporting to real business decisions, it becomes a control and execution challenge. Explore how you can replace manual processes with AI for trusted, scalable insights and decision support, delivering accountability for operational and financial decision-making.

Sophia Mendelsohn, SAP

Wednesday
11:30 am – 12:15 pm PUX1195

Creating AI-powered UX for your own applications with SAP Design System

Learn how to create AI-powered UX for your applications by using SAP Design System (SAP Fiori UX for web and mobile apps) with its powerful set of reusable components, patterns, and floorplans. See how building applications is made easier with our AI-enabled UI technologies for web and mobile apps.

Sophia Levens, SAP

Frederic Berg, SAP

Wednesday
11:30 am – 12:15 pm ERP2238

Western Sugar’s success with SAP Cloud ERP

Five years after go-live, Western Sugar Cooperative calls SAP Cloud ERP applications “wonderful.” Hear about why it chose public cloud; the value of clean core, best practices, strong configuration and integration, and continuous improvement; its smooth move from a two- to three-system landscape; and readiness for AI-enabled innovation.

 

Wednesday
11:30 am – 12:15 pm FOU2337

When AI becomes the interface to the enterprise

For decades, SAP helped shape enterprise software based on screens and transactions. Today, AI is evolving how we interact with systems. Explore how SAP leads this shift—uniting applications, data, and AI—and what this next phase of transformation means for organizations turning AI into a real competitive advantage.

Florian Heretsch, SAP

Wednesday
1:00 pm – 1:45 pm FOU1557

Getting started with SAP Build MCPs: Create your first CAP app and process

 Experience the powerful Model Context Protocol (MCP) integration in SAP Build solutions. Produce your first SAP Cloud Application Programming Model (CAP) application and SAP Fiori elements UI, while orchestrating workflows. See how AI-enhanced context improves coding efficiency and helps you build scalable, process-rich apps.

 

Tim Back, SAP

Jason Gabor, SAP

Wednesday
1:00 pm – 1:45 pm BTM1232

The AI-augmented architect: Upgrading architecture in the age of AI 

 AI is reshaping enterprise architecture. To provide value, enterprise architectures must embrace AI tools. Discover SAP technology leveraging AI for enhanced visibility and coordination. Through real-world examples, see how Joule and agentic AI increase information velocity and augment architect capabilities to thrive in this evolving landscape.

Martin Mysyk, SAP

Wednesday
2:00 pm – 2:20 pm PUX1210

AI and the future of user experience in SAP software

Prepare yourself for the upcoming revolution in user experience by understanding how we are harnessing AI to help users achieve better outcomes. Discover how AI is changing the idea of what an application is—and what this means for you. Learn about our AI design patterns and get a glimpse of the future SAP software user experience.

Emil Voutta, SAP

Wednesday
2:00 pm – 2:20 pm JOU2647

The future of enterprise applications and AI experiences

The enterprise application is no longer a system of record but a system of action. As AI moves from assistant to agent, organizations shift from what their software can store to what it can do. Learn why the future belongs to applications that don't just surface information but understand context, anticipate decisions, and act with confidence.

 

Wednesday
3:00 pm – 3:20 pm PUX1194

User experience innovations and road map for SAP Business Suite

Have a look at the latest user experience innovations across our suite of applications, along with exciting web and mobile innovations coming soon. Also check out selected highlights from Ul technologies for creating great UX for your own applications.

Tom Reiss, SAP

I’m excited to hear what we announce in the keynotes and experience the future of enterprise software. AI will transform how we all interact with apps. The future starts now.

Here are some other summaries of Sapphire sessions from other colleagues.

Inside SAP Build at SAP Sapphire 2026: What to Expect from AI Agents and Agentic Development by Mark Wright

See you in Orlando!

For the SAP Cloud ERP UX Foundation team, Peter Spielvogel.

entry.title: SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition: CPFH Sales Kits in Consignment
entry.content:
Introduction

With release 2402, support for Sales Kits of type CPFH in consignment processing was delivered.

At first sight, this may look like a limited scope enhancement: allowing a Sales Kit to be used in consignment scenarios. In reality, the change is much more significant. The real requirement was not just order entry support, but a process model that preserves the commercial meaning of the kit while still allowing the system to execute the logistics and inventory steps on component level.

This is exactly where CPFH becomes relevant.

In a CPFH Sales Kit, the processing logic is clearly split:

  • the main item carries the commercial meaning and pricing
  • the subitems carry the delivery- and inventory-relevant execution

This split is particularly important in consignment processing, because consignment is not just a sales-document-driven scenario. It is a stock-oriented business process with distinct follow-up steps:

  • Consignment Fill-Up
  • Consignment Issue
  • Consignment Pick-Up
  • Consignment Return

For a normal material, these steps are straightforward. For a Sales Kit, they are not. The customer orders one commercial object, but the system needs to execute on multiple physical components.

This blog explains how CPFH Sales Kits are processed in consignment, what the business meaning of the relevant item categories is, and how the solution integrates into the standard consignment execution framework.

Business Background

Among the different Sales Kit types, CPFH is especially suitable for consignment because it already reflects the separation that consignment processing needs:

  • commercial meaning on header level
  • execution meaning on component level

From a business perspective, this allows the system to preserve the kit as the object the customer sees and buys.

From an execution perspective, it allows the system to process the components that are physically delivered, consumed, picked up, or returned.

Without such a split, a kit might still look correct in the sales document, but the downstream process would become inconsistent. The document would show one object, while the actual stock movements would need another.

The core design principle is therefore simple:

The header keeps the kit semantics. The subitems carry the consignment execution semantics.

Scope Delivered in 2402

The current delivery scope focuses on Sales Kits of type CPFH for the following consignment process steps:

  1. Consignment Fill-Up
  2. Consignment Issue
  3. Consignment Pick-Up
  4. Consignment Return

Across all four steps, the solution distinguishes between:

  • header item categories, which define the business role of the kit header
  • subitem item categories, which define the execution role of the components

This distinction is essential. The different item categories are not technical duplicates. They exist because the business meaning of the kit header is not the same in Fill-Up, Issue, Pick-Up, and Return.

Overall Processing Logic

The following table summarizes the end-to-end logic of CPFH in consignment.

Table 1: CPFH in Consignment – Overall Logic

Business Step Header Item Category Header Meaning Subitem Item Category Subitem Meaning Schedule Line Movement Type
Fill-UpCF3HKit header, preserves kit semantics, not used as a normal revenue-relevant billing itemCF3IComponents moved into customer consignment stockE1 / E0631
IssueCPHDPriced and billing-relevant kit headerCI3LComponents actually consumed / issuedC1 / C0633
IssueCFHDNot priced on header, but still billing-relevantCI3LComponents actually consumed / issuedC1 / C0633
IssueCNHDNon-priced, non-billing header, used only to preserve kit structureCI3FComponents actually consumed / issuedC1 / C0633
Pick-UpCP3HKit header for pick-up processingCP3IComponents picked up from customer consignment stockF1 / F0632
ReturnCR3PPriced and billing-relevant return headerCR3LComponents processed in returnD0634
ReturnCR3FNot priced on header, but still billing-relevantCR3LComponents processed in returnD0634
ReturnCR3NNon-priced, non-billing header, used only to preserve kit structureCR3IComponents processed in returnD0634

This table highlights the key principle of the solution:

  • the header categories differentiate the business role of the kit header
  • the subitem categories carry the actual execution logic
  • the stock-relevant processing always happens on subitem level

Consignment Fill-Up

Business Meaning

In Consignment Fill-Up, stock is moved to customer consignment stock.
At this stage, the customer has not yet actually consumed the goods. Therefore, Fill-Up is not a revenue event. It is much closer to a stock transfer into customer consignment stock.

That business meaning is reflected in the item category design.

Header: CF3H

The CPFH header item category in Fill-Up is CF3H.

Its purpose is to:

  • preserve the Sales Kit header in the document
  • keep the commercial representation of the kit
  • provide the structural basis for the exploded subitems

At the same time, CF3H is not the execution-relevant stock item. And because Fill-Up is not the point of revenue realization, it is not intended for normal billing logic. In practice, it fits pro forma invoice semantics rather than standard billing.

So the role of CF3H is not to execute the stock movement, but to keep the kit visible and meaningful in the process.

Subitem: CF3I

The actual execution in Fill-Up happens on CF3I.

CF3I represents:

  • the exploded component line
  • the material actually moved into customer consignment stock
  • the execution-relevant line for Fill-Up

It is mapped to:

  • E1 / E0
  • movement type 631

So the Fill-Up logic can be summarized as:

CF3H keeps the kit header. CF3I performs the actual fill-up execution.

Consignment Issue

Issue is the most commercially relevant step in consignment, because this is the point where the customer actually consumes stock from customer consignment inventory.

This is why the system distinguishes between multiple header item categories for Issue. They are not redundant. They reflect different business meanings of the kit header.

CPHD – Priced and Billing-Relevant Issue Header

CPHD represents the most direct commercial Issue path.

Its meaning is:

  • the header is priced
  • the header is billing-relevant
  • the kit has entered a commercial settlement path

The corresponding execution-relevant subitem is CI3L.

So CPHD + CI3L represents:

A commercially relevant kit header combined with component-level issue execution.

CFHD – Not Priced on Header, but Still Billing-Relevant

CFHD differs from CPHD.

Here, the header does not behave like a standard priced header item, but it still remains billing-relevant. This makes CFHD an alternative Issue path where commercial relevance is preserved, but header pricing is not the defining characteristic.

The corresponding subitem is also CI3L.

CNHD – Non-Priced and Non-Billing Issue Header

CNHD is especially important to understand.

Its role is:

  • no pricing
  • no billing
  • the header exists only to preserve the structural semantics of the kit

This means CNHD is not a commercial settlement header. It is a structural header. The document still needs a valid kit header, even though the actual execution happens on the components.

The corresponding subitem is CI3F.

So CNHD + CI3F represents:

A purely structural Issue header with actual component-level execution.

Subitems in Issue: CI3L and CI3F

The actual Issue execution is always done on the subitems, not on the header.

The relevant subitem categories are:

  • CI3L
  • CI3F

Both are mapped to:

  • C1 / C0
  • movement type 633

Their common role is clear:

They carry the actual component-level consumption of consignment stock.

Consignment Pick-Up

Consignment Pick-Up retrieves stock from customer consignment stock that has not yet been consumed.

Again, the business document keeps the kit header, but execution happens on the components.

Header: CP3H

CP3H is the CPFH header item category for Pick-Up.

Its purpose is to:

  • preserve the kit structure in the document
  • keep the kit header visible in pick-up processing
  • provide the structural basis for the component lines

Subitem: CP3I

The actual Pick-Up execution happens on CP3I.

It represents:

  • the components being retrieved
  • the stock-relevant line in pick-up processing

It is mapped to:

  • F1 / F0
  • movement type 632

So Pick-Up follows the same overall principle:

CP3H preserves the kit meaning, while CP3I performs the actual component-level retrieval.

Consignment Return

Return processing again differentiates between multiple header paths, because not every return header has the same commercial meaning.

CR3P – Priced and Billing-Relevant Return Header

CR3P represents:

  • a priced header
  • a billing-relevant return path
  • a commercially relevant return header for the kit

The corresponding subitem is CR3L.

So CR3P + CR3L means:

A commercially relevant return header with actual component-level return execution.

CR3F – Not Priced on Header, but Still Billing-Relevant

CR3F is conceptually similar to CFHD in Issue.

Its meaning is:

  • the header is not priced in the normal way
  • but it remains billing-relevant

The corresponding subitem is also CR3L.

CR3N – Non-Priced and Non-Billing Return Header

CR3N is the Return counterpart of CNHD.

Its role is:

  • no pricing
  • no billing
  • the header exists only to preserve the structural semantics of the kit

The corresponding execution-relevant subitem is CR3I.

So CR3N + CR3I means:

A purely structural return header with actual component-level return execution.

Subitems in Return: CR3L and CR3I

The actual Return execution happens on:

  • CR3L
  • CR3I

These are mapped to:

  • D0
  • movement type 634

So even in Return, the business pattern remains unchanged:

The header keeps the kit meaning, while the subitem performs the actual return execution.

Relationship to Standard Consignment Item Categories

A helpful way to understand the design is to compare the CPFH subitems with the standard consignment item categories.

Table 2: Standard vs. CPFH Execution Paths

Standard Item Category Business Step CPFH Subitem Item Category Meaning
CFNFill-UpCF3IStandard fill-up item vs. CPFH component fill-up item
CIN / CCIN / CCXNIssueCI3L / CI3FStandard issue item vs. CPFH component issue item
CPNPick-UpCP3IStandard pick-up item vs. CPFH component pick-up item
CRN / CRBN / CCRNReturnCR3L / CR3IStandard return item vs. CPFH component return item

This comparison makes one design principle very clear:

The solution does not create a new consignment execution model. It adds a header/subitem split on top of the standard consignment execution patterns.

In other words:

  • for a normal material, the system directly uses the standard item category path
  • for a CPFH kit, the system keeps the kit header and lets the subitems reuse the standard execution behavior

Technical Design Perspective

From a technical point of view, the implementation can be understood in three layers.

1. Item Category Determination

The system first determines the header item category, and then derives the subitem item category based on the higher-level item category.

This allows the document to preserve the kit structure while still generating execution-relevant component lines.

2. Business Role of Header and Subitem

The header item categories distinguish whether the kit header is:

  • priced
  • billing-relevant
  • or purely structural

The subitem item categories distinguish the actual execution role of the components for:

  • Fill-Up
  • Issue
  • Pick-Up
  • Return

3. Schedule Line Determination and Movement Execution

The subitems are then mapped back to the standard consignment execution framework:

  • Fill-Up: E1 / E0 → 631
  • Issue: C1 / C0 → 633
  • Pick-Up: F1 / F0 → 632
  • Return: D0 → 634

So the real innovation of the solution is not a new movement model. The innovation is:

making the CPFH kit structure executable within the standard consignment framework.

Roadmap Outlook

With the delivery of CPFH support in consignment processing in 2402, the first step has been taken to enable Sales Kits in this area.

Support for additional Sales Kit types, including ERLA, DISH, and LUMF, is planned to be introduced gradually in future releases after 2702, further expanding the overall coverage of Sales Kits in consignment scenarios.

Conclusion

Supporting CPFH in consignment is not just about allowing a Sales Kit in a consignment order. It addresses a more fundamental business and technical requirement:

How can a Sales Kit remain a kit from a commercial perspective, while still being executed consistently on component level in consignment processing?

The answer is based on three principles:

  1. The header keeps the kit semantics
    Different header item categories distinguish whether the header is priced, billing-relevant, or purely structural.
  2. The subitems carry the execution semantics
    The real inventory and movement-relevant processing happens on the component-level subitems.
  3. The execution layer reuses the standard consignment framework
    Standard schedule line categories and movement types are reused, so CPFH can run consistently within existing consignment processing.

With the 2402 delivery, CPFH Sales Kits can now be processed in consignment while preserving both:

  • the commercial representation of the kit
  • the execution logic of the components

This also lays the groundwork for further support of additional Sales Kit types in consignment scenarios in future releases.

entry.title: Dynamic Watermark in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud Output Management: A Step-by-Step Guide
entry.content:
Recently, I got one of those requirements that sounds incredibly simple on paper but turns into an interesting technical puzzle once you work on S4HPC.

The business wanted a conditional watermark on their Purchase Order Output Form. If the PO is still navigating the approval workflow, it needs to display a massive "DRAFT" watermark across all pages. Once fully approved and released, that watermark needs to disappear completely.

The idea is straightforward: add custom logic to make the watermark conditional at both, form template level and the data source level. But as anyone who works with SAP Output Management knows, getting the Master Form and Content Form to talk to each other isn't always plug-and-play.

Here is how I did it.

Step 1: The Data Source Dilemma

Since the watermark needs to appear on all pages, the logical place to put it is the master form template.

First, I checked the standard PO form data source FDP_EF_PURCHASE_ORDER_SRV to see if I could simply grab the PurchasingProcessingStatus and ReleaseIsNotCompleted fields of I_PurchaseOrderAPI01 view to build my condition. Unsurprisingly, they weren't there.

My next thought was to extend the standard data source via the Custom Fields app. However, FDP_EF_PURCHASE_ORDER_SRV cannot be extended.

After some digging, I came across SAP Note 3525721, which states that custom fields can be used for the PO output data source with the use BAdI MMPUR_PO_OUTPUT_HEADER_EXT.

 

Step 2: Adding the Custom Field via BAdI 

First, I went to the Custom Fields app (Business Context: Procurement: Purchasing Document) and created a new Text(1) or Indicator field called YY1_PO_WF_APPROVED_PDH. I made sure to enable its usage for the respective Form Templates.

Next, I implemented the BAdI MMPUR_PO_OUTPUT_HEADER_EXT to determine the workflow status. Here is the BAdI logic:

SELECT SINGLE FROM i_purchaseorderapi01 WITH PRIVILEGED ACCESS
  FIELDS 
  WHERE purchaseorder = @purchaseorder
    AND releaseisnotcompleted = @ABAP_false
    AND purchasingprocessingstatus = '05'
  INTO (po_approved).
IF sy-subrc = 0.
  purdoc_extension-yy1_po_wf_approved_pdh = 'X'.
ENDIF.

 After downloading the Content Form XML/XDP again, the newly added custom field was sitting right there in the Data View palette.

Step 3: Form Layout Setup

Now, let’s jump into Adobe LiveCycle Designer to actually build the layout.

I added the DRAFT image inside a subform (named formWatermark) on both of the Master Pages: mst1 (the first page) and mst2 (subsequent pages).

Note: Make sure your formWatermark subform is set to Positioned (not Flowed) in the Object palette, and right-click to select Send to Back. This ensures it acts as a true background watermark and doesn't push your main content areas down.

Here is where we hit the major roadblock: How do we access the master page watermark subform from the content form? Or conversely, how do we access the content form data from the master page?

This can be achieved from either side using a simple xfa.resolveNode trick. You only need to use one of the following methods/approaches.

Note: For both approaches, ensure your Script Editor dropdowns are set to Language: JavaScript and Run At: Server. In S/4HANA Public Cloud, form rendering happens on the Server. 

Method #1: Controlling it from the Master Template Form

To access our custom field value from the Content template form dataset within the Master template form, we need the absolute path of the field from the Content template's data view (e.g., PurchaseOrderNode.YY1_PO_WF_APPROVED_PDH).

We concatenate xfa.datasets.data.Form. with our custom field path. Place this JavaScript logic on the formWatermark subform's initialize event:

var WFStatusNode = xfa.resolveNode("xfa.datasets.data.Form.PurchaseOrderNode.YY1_PO_WF_APPROVED_PDH");

if (WFStatusNode.value === "X") {
    this.presence = "hidden";
} else {
    this.presence = "visible";
}

Method #2: Controlling it from the Content Template Form

Alternatively, we can access the master page watermark subforms directly from within the content template form by prefixing the path with #pageSet[0]..

Place this JavaScript code on the YY1_PO_WF_APPROVED_PDH field's initialize event:

var firstPageWatermarkNode = xfa.resolveNode("#pageSet[0].mst1.formWatermark");
var subsequentPageWatermarkNode = xfa.resolveNode("#pageSet[0].mst2.formWatermark");

if (this.rawValue === "X") {
    firstPageWatermarkNode.presence = "hidden";
    subsequentPageWatermarkNode.presence = "hidden";
} else {
    firstPageWatermarkNode.presence = "visible";
    subsequentPageWatermarkNode.presence = "visible";
}

The Final Result

After choosing your preferred approach, upload the forms (Master and Content) back into the system and test your output.

When the PO is still in approval:

And once the approval is fully completed:

The draft watermark completely vanishes!

It’s a relatively simple requirement that forces you to touch a few different areas of S/4HANA Cloud Extensibility, combining the MMPUR_PO_OUTPUT_HEADER_EXT BAdI with a little Adobe Forms JavaScript gets the job done perfectly.

Have you tackled similar form requirements in Public Cloud recently? Let me know your approach in the comments.

entry.title: Introducing the Success Guide for SAP Business Suite: Enabling Transformation and Project Success
entry.content:
We are excited to announce the release of the Success Guide – Project Experience Sharing for SAP Business Suite, aligned with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2602.

This release marks a significant milestone in the evolution of the Success Guide. What began as focused guidance for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition has now expanded into a Business Suite – oriented framework, offering broader, deeper, and more integrated insights to support complex enterprise transformation journeys.

A Strategic Shift Toward Business Suite Integration

As customers increasingly adopt a suite-first strategy, implementation success depends on more than mastering a single product. It requires a holistic understanding of how solutions interact across the enterprise landscape.

The Success Guide reflects this shift by extending beyond SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition to include cross-solution integration guidance across key SAP Business Suite components, such as:

  • SAP Cloud ERP
  • SAP Concur
  • SAP Ariba and SAP Business Network
  • ....

By consolidating insights across these solutions, the guide enables project teams to move from product-level expertise to end-to-end solution thinking, ensuring more cohesive implementations and reduced integration risks.

Built on Real Project Experience and Cross-Team Expertise

The Success Guide is not just theoretical documentation - it is grounded in real-world project experience and built through extensive collaboration across multiple SAP teams. By combining field learnings, delivery experience, and product insights, the guide captures recurring risk patterns, proven mitigation strategies, and actionable recommendations that can be directly applied in implementation projects.

This collective intelligence transforms the guide into a practical decision-support tool for project teams, rather than a static reference document.

Designed to Mitigate Risk and Accelerate Delivery

A core objective of the Success Guide is to proactively address common implementation challenges.

It provides:

  • Identified project risk points and root causes
  • Recommended mitigation actions and best practices
  • Contextualized insights aligned to SAP Activate phases
  • Integration-specific considerations across Business Suite solutions

By leveraging these insights early - especially during the Prepare and Explore phases - teams can significantly reduce rework, avoid late-stage surprises, and maintain project momentum.

Continuous Innovation Through Structured Updates

To remain relevant in a fast-evolving cloud landscape, the Success Guide follows a biannual update cycle, aligned with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition major releases.

Each update incorporates:

  • Lessons learned from recent implementation projects
  • Enhancements to existing guidance based on product evolution
  • Expanded coverage across new scenarios and integrations

Additionally, the document benefits from AI-assisted content validation, improving consistency, accuracy, and scalability of the knowledge base.

This ensures the guide remains current, actionable, and aligned with real customer needs.

More Than Documentation: A Trusted Companion for Project Success

The Success Guide is designed to be more than a knowledge repository. It acts as a trusted companion throughout the project lifecycle, helping teams to:

  • Reduce implementation risk
  • Accelerate time-to-value
  • Improve cross-team alignment
  • Leverage proven experience at scale

Whether you are a consultant, architect, or project manager, the guide provides experience-driven insights to support confident decision-making at every stage.

Access the Success Guide

The latest version of the Success Guide is available via the SAP Digital Library – Success Guide, providing centralized and curated access to all updated content.

We encourage all project team members to leverage this guide early and continuously to maximize project outcomes and drive successful SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition as well as SAP Business Suite implementations.

entry.title: Preliminary What’s New Information for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2602.2
entry.content:
Preliminary version of What’s New information and of the Product Assistance are now available for customers and partners to get a preview on the upcoming new and changed features of SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2602.2.

You can find this preview on the current SAP Help Portal page SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition (see What’s New section “Preview of SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2602.2”). To access the Preliminary What’s New information, please use the What’s New Viewer or the Release Assessment and Scope Dependency (RASD) tool.

The RASD tool provides you with a personalized and tailored view on What’s New information based on the activated and used scope within your system. The preliminary information for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2602.2 will be available in RASD within the next few days. We recommend using RASD to prepare for upgrades and updates of your system and to focus on the new and changed features that are relevant for you. A valid S-User ID is needed to use the RASD tool and to view all the preliminary content stated above.

For any feedback you may have, try the Commenting Feature for What's New documentation in this release. See this blog for more information.

entry.title: Solution Order Management in SAP Cloud ERP 2602
entry.content:
The SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2602 update continues our commitment to delivering intelligent, flexible, and user-centric enhancements. This update introduces powerful new capabilities specifically designed to streamline processes for Solution Order Specialists. New capabilities have been introduced covering the full scope of solution selling with SAP Cloud ERP, and in this blog, we’ll look at them in more detail.

Customer Hierarchy and Product Hierarchy related Pricing in Solution Orders

 

You can now leverage product hierarchies directly within the Manage Solution Orders app. Product hierarchies are a cornerstone among the generic features for organizing products, or materials, across multiple levels. This structured approach is particularly valuable for complex pricing scenarios. When your price calculations are configured to depend on product hierarchy data, you can now use these hierarchies in your solution orders for precise and automated price determination, simplifying a critical part of the sales process.

Similar to the enhancement for products, you can now utilize customer hierarchies in the Manage Solution Orders app. Organizing customers into multi-level hierarchies is often essential for managing pricing agreements and sales strategies. With this feature, you can now use customer hierarchies within solution orders for price determination, provided your pricing procedures are set up to recognize this data. This enables more accurate, automated pricing tailored to your customer structures.

For an in-depth look at customer and product hierarchies in solution order management, take a look at this blog

Sales Item Enhancements in 2602

Enhanced Flexibility with New Item Categories and Process Variants

As a solution order specialist, compiling sales orders that meet specific business needs and align with customer agreements is essential. This requires a high degree of flexibility, With 2602, we are extending solution orders to cover sales processes that were previously unsupported for configurable products and sales kits. The Manage Solution Orders app is made more versatile with the introduction of new item categories and process variants, opening the door for selling high-value, make-to-order items.

 



From 2602, we support milestone billing in solution orders. Milestone billing is handled with a billing plan that splits total sales-order value across predefined billing dates, instead of billing everything at once -typically used for long duration deliveries or projects. Introducing this capability also applies milestone-based billing to established sales kit scenarios such as ERLA. LUMF and CPFH. Furthermore, it provides solution order specialists with the option to manage down payments as part of solution selling.

 

Alongside milestone-based billing, we introduce the ability to run advanced down payment (ADP) processing for sales orders that are created as follow-ups to solution orders. This enhancement integrates your solution order process more tightly with your financial operations, securing revenue upfront for large or custom orders. Before utilizing this feature, a configuration expert must ensure the condition types DDPR and DDPS have been added to the pricing procedure used for your solution orders.

New item categories introduced to the manage solution orders app support third-party sales with variant configuration. We have expanded solution order capabilities for configurable products by adding support for third-party shipment scenarios. This should help business users handle a broader range of sales scenarios within a single framework and better orchestrate complex fulfillment models involving external suppliers. This enhancement makes it possible to manage third-party sales in make-to-order processes, both with and without charge, giving companies more flexibility in how they structure customer-specific solution offerings.

 

Overall, these changes strengthen solution selling by enabling more adaptable end-to-end sales processes. They also improve business-user productivity by making it easier to manage customer-specific requirements through a more flexible and comprehensive solution order process.

This update is critical for businesses that manage high-value sales items, as it helps optimize cash flow through milestone-based invoicing. It also delivers a more flexible, customer-centric approach to sales order processing by better accommodating complex product configurations in third-party scenarios.

See the documentation for a full overview of new item categories

 

Set Billing Blocks for Release and Completed Sales Items (2602.1)

A helpful enhancement in the Manage Solution Orders app now lets users set or reset billing blocks even after sales items have reached Released or Completed status. That means billing issues can be corrected later in the process without reopening documents, helping teams work more efficiently and keep billing moving. Billing blocks are maintained by specifying the relevant billing block reason. Previously, this was only possible while sales items were still Open or In Process. There are a couple of important conditions to keep in mind: this feature is available for sales items released for follow-up sales orders of type OR, while items released for sales orders of type SOR have always allowed billing block updates. The same capability is also available through the business solution order APIs, including API_BUS_SOLUTION_ORDER_SRV and BusinessSolutionOrder.

Cumulated net value field for solution order items in the solution order output form

 

Solution bundles let you sell a combined offering in a solution order using one bundle item with predefined subitems such as products, services, projects, contracts, or subscriptions. SAP treats the bundle item as a configurable product and, after release, generates the relevant follow-up documents for the underlying subitems. When selling preconfigured bundles, customers wish to indicate the bundle price in the order confirmation they issue to customers to provide more transparency. As a result, with 2602, we make a cumulated net value field available in the output interface and forms. This shows the aggregated price of the purchased bundle without a breakdown of the individual items in the bundle.

 

Transfer of Transactional Address to Sales Orders

To provide greater flexibility in managing address information for complex business scenarios, business users can now define specific transactional addresses for business partners (category as organization) at the solution order level. This address information is then seamlessly transferred to any subsequent sales orders created from that solution order.

Note: While a change of address on subsequent documents is permitted and will be reflected throughout the document chain, this action is recommended only for making minor typographical corrections to maintain data consistency.


Filter by Solution Order When Scheduling Billing Document Creation (2508.1)

 

A feature which was released in the first micro-delivery of the 2508 release which we want to draw your attention to is the support of scheduled billing creation with solution orders. Previously, filtering by solution order was only available in the Create Billing Documents app. Now, the same capability is available in Schedule Billing Creation, allowing users either to select billing-relevant documents for specific solution orders or to include all solution-order-related billing due list items in a recurring job. This supports both specialized solution-selling scenarios, where only selected solution orders should be billed, and broader operational setups where solution-order processing is used more widely alongside other sales scenarios.

To support this, we have introduced a new Solution Order field under SD Document Data in the app. As part of the UI update, the SD Document field was also moved under SD Document Data, and Documents to Be Selected was renamed to SD Documents to Be Selected. Overall, this enhancement helps organizations automate invoicing for solution-order-based business more efficiently, reduce manual effort, and process larger billing volumes faster.

 

Filtered Date Rules for Service Contract Billing in Solution Orders

We have increased clarity and convenience in billing plan management. You can now use filtered date rules, configured by key users, for the billing plan of service contract items in solution orders. This applies to both settlement period rules and billing date rules. Previously, the Manage Solution Orders app displayed all available rules, which required users to rely on descriptive texts to find the correct one. This new, filtered approach ensures that only relevant rules are presented, significantly reducing errors and improving user efficiency.

Subscription Items Enhancements in Solution Order Management (2508.3)

Selling subscriptions within solution orders has been significantly enhanced in the 2508 microdelivery releases, and further with 2602. Below, you’ll find an overview of key new capabilities introduced to make subscription selling more reliable, flexible, and user-friendly.


Subscription Validation in Solution Orders (2508.3)

One of the most valuable updates is support for the subscription validation extension point from SAP Subscription Billing. This helps business users prevent the creation of incorrect or incomplete follow-up subscriptions by validating subscription data during simulation and release. As a result, errors can be caught earlier in the process, improving productivity and reducing the need for later corrections.

 


Auto-Locking for subscription item prices (2508.3)

A major usability improvement is the new Auto-Lock pricing capability. With this feature, prices can be locked automatically once a subscription is created, protecting the agreed subscription price from later changes in the underlying product list price. In the Solution Order UI, each price element now shows a Customization Status, indicating whether it is customizable or non-customizable, along with a new Auto-Lock column that shows whether the price is currently locked. This gives users much better transparency into pricing behavior and helps improve customer satisfaction by ensuring stable subscription pricing over time.

The enhancement supports three pricing scenarios: customizable only, auto-locked only, and both customizable and auto-locked. When both settings are enabled, the price can be customized once, after which the auto-lock is removed. These capabilities are powered by new fields in the subscription API, which provide the necessary status information and ensure a more consistent and intuitive user experience across the process.

The release also increases flexibility in commercial subscription models by adding support for both credit and charge rate elements. This means businesses can now model one-time credits as well as one-time charges directly in Solution Order, enabling more creative and customer-friendly offerings such as onboarding credits, promotional incentives, or service compensation.

Alternative currencies for subscription items

Subscriptions can now be created in an alternate market currency, making it easier to satisfy customer-specific regional or contractual requirements when the subscription currency differs from the main Solution Order transaction currency. Together, these enhancements make subscription handling more robust, transparent, and adaptable for real-world solution selling.

 

Integration

Simulation API for Solution Orders for external Quoting applications

Building on the 2508 release, we have made further enhancements to the Solution Order API with OData V4 simulation capabilities that allow businesses to simulate solution orders without creating actual transactional documents. This gives external quoting and sales applications access to SAP Cloud ERP logic in real time, making it easier to generate accurate quotes based on partner determination, pricing, and billing plan simulation.

The API returns detailed simulation results at both header and item level, including net value, partners, price elements, and contract billing plans for solution orders and their items. It supports a broad range of item types, such as sales items, service contract items, and service order items, while also covering scenarios like sales kits, service bundles, and preconfigured solution bundles.

These capabilities improve integration flexibility and support more seamless orchestration across external sales tools and SAP Cloud ERP. With built-in extensibility and secure OAuth 2.0-based integration, the Solution Order API provides a strong foundation for connected sales processes, while future roadmap plans include support for configurable products, parameterized solution bundles, and subscription products.

 

 


Extensibility Enhancements

Enable Subscription billing cloud BAdI for developer extensibility

Extensibility is a critical success factor in customer implementation projects, especially when standard capabilities in solution business and service management need to be enhanced to meet specific business requirements. Recent innovations strengthen both developer and side-by-side extensibility. On the developer extensibility side, READ access through Entity Manipulation Language (EML) is now available for BAdI CL_CRMS4_SERV_CHCK_BEFORE_SAVE, enabling the use of RAP façades to access the transient data buffer introduced in release 2602. In addition, BAdI CRMS4_SOLO_ORC_SB_SUBSC_MODIFY has now been officially released, giving developers more flexibility to adapt subscription-related processes. For side-by-side extensibility, “derived” events based on standard events make it possible to enrich event payloads and define context attributes for more precise filtering. This capability is already available for Service Contract in 2602, with Solution Order support planned from 2608 onward.



 

 Find all our assets

Do you want to learn more about the main innovations in different lines of business in the SAP Cloud ERP 2602 release? The release 2602 showcases more 130 innovation highlights across the portfolio. Check out our release document for all the details!

 

Enablement

We at Cloud ERP and SCM Product Success offer a service as versatile as our product itself. Click on the enablement wheel below and check out the numerous offerings our team has created for you: 

 

 

 

 ️ Watch the replays of our SAP Cloud ERP 2602 Early Release Series!

From January 12-16, we hosted a series of 22 expert-led live sessions to highlight the exciting innovations shipped with the SAP Cloud ERP 2602 release. Missed the live sessions? We've got you covered! Take advantage of the entire series or pick and choose the sessions most relevant to you. You will find the recording and presentation for Asset Management as well as 21 other topics of interest. Don't miss this chance and watch them on demand – anywhere, anytime.
